# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:22.04

# Install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    nginx \
    postgresql \
    redis-server \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Configure Nginx
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

# Configure PostgreSQL
RUN service postgresql start && \
    sudo -u postgres psql -c "ALTER USER postgres PASSWORD 'secure_password';" && \
    service postgresql stop

# Configure Redis
COPY redis.conf /etc/redis/redis.conf

# Create a non-root user
RUN useradd -m appuser
USER appuser

# Expose ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Start services
CMD ["sh", "-c", "service nginx start && service postgresql start && redis-server /etc/redis/redis.conf && tail -f /dev/null"]
